content:
# Titus Chapter 1 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), pages 222-223 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/TIT_001_page_001.jpg, TIT_001_page_002.jpg

---

**[1]** From Paul, a servant of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ — sent to strengthen the faith of God's chosen people and to deepen their knowledge of the truth that leads to a godly life, **[2]** resting on the hope of eternal life that God, who cannot lie, promised before time itself began, **[3]** and which he revealed at just the right moment through the proclamation entrusted to me by the command of God our Savior —

**[4]** To Titus, my true son in the faith we share: May grace and peace be yours from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Savior.

**[5]** I left you behind in Crete for this reason: to put in order what still needed to be set right, and to appoint elders in every town, following the guidelines I gave you. **[6]** An elder must be above reproach — faithful to his wife, with children who are believers and not open to charges of wild living or defiance. **[7]** Since an overseer manages God's household, he must be above reproach: not arrogant, not hot-tempered, not a heavy drinker, not violent, and not greedy for money. **[8]** Instead, he must be hospitable, a lover of what is good, self-controlled, upright, devout, and disciplined. **[9]** He must hold firmly to the trustworthy message as it has been taught, so that he is able both to encourage others with sound teaching and to expose those who contradict it.

**[10]** For there are many rebellious people out there — idle talkers and deceivers — especially those from the circumcision party. **[11]** They must be silenced, because they are ruining entire households by teaching things they have no business teaching, and they do it for shameful profit. **[12]** One of their own prophets said it: "Cretans are always liars, vicious beasts, and lazy gluttons." **[13]** And that testimony is accurate! So confront them sharply, so that they become healthy in the faith **[14]** and stop devoting themselves to Jewish myths and to the commands of people who turn their backs on the truth.

**[15]** To the pure, everything is pure. But to those who are corrupt and faithless, nothing is pure — even their minds and consciences are polluted. **[16]** They claim to know God, but their actions tell a different story. They are detestable, disobedient, and worthless for doing anything good.

---

## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Verse 1 — "sent to strengthen... and to deepen":** The Greek reads ΚΑΤΑ ΠΙΣΤΙΝ ΕΚΛΕΚΤΩΝ ΘΕΟΥ ΚΑΙ ΕΠΙΓΝΩΣΙΝ ΑΛΗΘΕΙΑΣ ("according to the faith of God's elect and the knowledge of truth"). The preposition ΚΑΤΑ here expresses purpose and alignment. Rather than the wooden "according to," I rendered the purposive force: Paul's apostleship exists *to serve* the faith and knowledge of God's people. This captures the dynamic relationship Paul is describing.

**Verse 2 — "before time itself began":** The Greek ΠΡΟ ΧΡΟΝΩΝ ΑΙΩΝΙΩΝ literally reads "before eternal times" — a phrase that strains against its own logic (how can there be "times" before time?). "Before time itself began" captures the intended sense: God's promise precedes all created reality.

**Verse 6 — "faithful to his wife":** ΜΙΑΣ ΓΥΝΑΙΚΟΣ ΑΝΗΡ literally means "a one-woman man." Rather than the ambiguous "husband of one wife," I chose "faithful to his wife," which conveys the character quality Paul is after — marital faithfulness — rather than inviting debates about remarriage or polygamy that the phrase was not designed to adjudicate.

**Verse 10 — "especially those from the circumcision party":** ΜΑΛΙΣΤΑ ΟΙ ΕΚ ΤΗΣ ΠΕΡΙΤΟΜΗΣ — rendered as "circumcision party" rather than simply "the circumcision" to make clear this refers to a faction insisting on Jewish law observance, not to Jewish people generally.

**Verse 12 — "Cretans are always liars...":** This is a quotation attributed to Epimenides of Crete (6th century BCE). The manuscript reads ΚΡΗΤΕΣ ΑΕΙ ΨΕΥΣΤΑΙ ΚΑΚΑ ΘΗΡΙΑ ΓΑΣΤΕΡΕΣ ΑΡΓΑΙ. I rendered ΚΑΚΑ ΘΗΡΙΑ as "vicious beasts" and ΓΑΣΤΕΡΕΣ ΑΡΓΑΙ as "lazy gluttons" — the latter literally means "idle bellies," a vivid insult that "lazy gluttons" captures well in English.

**Verse 15 — "everything is pure... nothing is pure":** The parallelism here is deliberately stark in Greek (ΠΑΝΤΑ ΚΑΘΑΡΑ... ΟΥΔΕΝ ΚΑΘΑΡΟΝ) and I preserved that starkness. The contrast is the whole point — purity is a condition of the person, not of external things.

**Verse 16 — "their actions tell a different story":** The Greek ΤΟΙΣ ΔΕ ΕΡΓΟΙΣ ΑΡΝΟΥΝΤΑΙ reads literally "but by their works they deny [him]." The dynamic rendering "their actions tell a different story" preserves the force of the contradiction between profession and practice, which is Paul's rhetorical thrust here.

**General note on sentence structure:** Verses 1-3 form a single, sprawling Greek sentence — Paul's greeting. I restructured it into flowing English while preserving the logical chain: Paul's identity leads to his purpose, which rests on a hope, which was promised and then revealed. The em dashes help English readers follow the parenthetical logic that Greek achieves through participial chains.

— PNEUMA
